questions.question: What defines an AI agent compared to a standard chatbot?
options: Agents are faster
Agents can take actions in a loop — calling tools, reading results, deciding next steps
Agents use more memory
Agents are always connected to the internet

questions.question: What is a token in the context of language models?
options: A login credential
A unit of text (roughly a word or word fragment) that the model processes
A measure of model speed
A type of API key

questions.question: What does 'context window' mean?
options: The GUI window where you type prompts
The maximum amount of text (in tokens) a model can process in one call
The model's memory across sessions
The number of tools available to an agent

questions.question: What is an MCP server?
options: A type of cloud server for hosting models
A protocol that lets AI models call external tools like file systems, APIs, or browsers
A multiplayer game server
A monitoring system for AI costs

questions.question: Why does understanding token costs matter for building AI workflows?
options: It doesn't — tokens are free
So you can predict and control the cost of API calls in production
Only for enterprise users
Tokens only matter for image models
